behaviors.description: The primary translator produces a grammatically coherent translation that preserves the meaning of the source text, not just a word-for-word substitution.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is explicitly about translation quality (grammar + meaning preservation), which depends on real model judgment and can vary across runs/prompts. Unit/integration tests with stubs would hide this. Workflow was considered but rejected because the behavior evaluates a single model output, not multiple model-dependent steps in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Call the real translation model on a representative multilingual corpus and assert quality thresholds (e.g., semantic similarity/human rubric for adequacy+fluency) rather than exact string match.

behaviors.description: When the translation API returns a 503 error, the service transparently falls back to the dictionary-based translator without returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: This is an external API error-path contract check (503 handling) plus externally visible service behavior (caller still gets successful translation). A pure mock can miss real HTTP/status handling nuances. Agentic was considered and rejected because correctness here is fallback orchestration on error, not model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run the service against a real HTTP endpoint (or controllable test server over real network) that returns 503, then verify the caller receives a successful fallback translation response and no propagated upstream error.

behaviors.description: The dictionary-based fallback returns the correct pre-stored translation for phrases that exist in the SQLite database.
behaviors.justification: Failure risk is module handoff between fallback translator and SQLite persistence/query layer. No real external API/OS contract beyond local DB wiring is required, and no AI output quality is involved. System was considered and rejected because behavior text only requires local SQLite retrieval correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Seed a real local SQLite test DB with known phrase mappings, invoke fallback translation path, and assert returned translations match stored values exactly.

behaviors.description: The quality tracking comparison correctly computes edit distance between primary and fallback outputs and stores the metric.
behaviors.justification: This combines deterministic algorithm correctness with persistence wiring (compute + store). Unit tests cover algorithm pieces, but integration is needed to catch compute-to-storage handoff issues. Agentic was considered and rejected because behavior does not require judging model output quality, only computing distance on given strings.
behaviors.test_description: Trigger comparison with controlled primary/fallback strings, verify computed character edit distance matches expected value, then read back persisted metric from storage to confirm it was saved correctly.

behaviors.description: When both translators are available, the service returns the primary translation and logs the quality comparison in the background.
behaviors.justification: This is orchestration/state-handoff: primary-result selection plus asynchronous quality-log side effect. It needs multi-module integration (translator selection + background logger + storage), but not real model-quality judgment. Agentic was considered and rejected because behavior text checks routing/side effects, not whether model content is good.
behaviors.test_description: With deterministic test doubles/fixtures for both translator outputs, call the service and assert immediate response equals primary output; then verify background quality metric/log entry appears via readback within timeout.
